metadata.subject: Mathematics
metadata.title: Cambridge IGCSE Mathematics 0580
metadata.description: Cambridge IGCSE Mathematics is tiered to enable effective differentiation for learners. The Core subject content is intended for learners targeting grades C–G, and the Extended subject content is intended for learners targeting grades A*–C. The Extended subject content contains the Core subject content as well as additional content.

subtopics.title: Types of number
content: Natural numbers
Integers (positive, zero and negative)
Prime numbers
Square numbers
Cube numbers
Common factors
Common multiples
Rational and irrational numbers
Reciprocals
examples: Convert between numbers and words (e.g., six billion is 6000000000, 10007 is ten thousand and seven)
Express 72 as a product of its prime factors
Find the highest common factor (HCF) of two numbers
Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of two numbers

subtopics.title: Sets
content: Set language, notation and Venn diagrams
Limited to two sets
notation: n(A) - Number of elements in set A
A' - Complement of set A
ξ - Universal set
A ∪ B - Union of A and B
A ∩ B - Intersection of A and B
examples: A = {x: x is a natural number}

subtopics.title: Powers and roots
content: Squares and square roots
Cubes and cube roots
Other powers and roots of numbers
required: Recall squares and roots from 1 to 15
Recall cubes and roots of 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and 10

subtopics.title: Fractions, decimals and percentages
content: Proper fractions
Improper fractions
Mixed numbers
Decimals
Percentages
Recognise equivalence and convert between forms
notes: Write fractions in simplest form
NOT expected: recurring decimal notation
NOT expected: converting recurring decimals to fractions

subtopics.title: Ordering
content: Order quantities by magnitude
Use symbols: =, ≠, >, <, ≥, ≤

subtopics.title: The four operations
content: Calculations with integers, fractions and decimals
Correct ordering of operations
Use of brackets
Negative numbers
Improper fractions and mixed numbers

subtopics.title: Indices I
content: Understand and use indices (positive, zero and negative integers)
Understand and use the rules of indices

subtopics.title: Standard form
content: Use standard form A × 10ⁿ where n is positive or negative integer and 1 ≤ A < 10
Convert numbers into and out of standard form
Calculate with values in standard form
notes: Core candidates expected to calculate with standard form only on Paper 3

subtopics.title: Estimation
content: Round values to specified degree of accuracy
Make estimates for calculations
Round answers to reasonable degree of accuracy
examples: Write 5764 correct to the nearest thousand
Estimate 41.3/(9.79 × 0.765) by rounding to 1 significant figure
notes: Includes decimal places and significant figures

subtopics.title: Limits of accuracy
content: Give upper and lower bounds for data rounded to specified accuracy
examples: Write down the upper bound of a length measured correct to the nearest metre
notes: NOT expected: bounds of results of calculations using rounded data

subtopics.title: Ratio and proportion
content: Give ratios in simplest form
Divide a quantity in a given ratio
Use proportional reasoning and ratios in context

subtopics.title: Rates
content: Use common measures of rate
Apply other measures of rate
Solve problems involving average speed
examples: A cyclist travels 45km in 3 hours 45 minutes. What is their average speed?
notes: Required formulas given in question
Knowledge of speed/distance/time formula required
Notation: m/s (metres per second), g/cm³ (grams per cubic centimetre)

subtopics.title: Percentages
content: Calculate a given percentage of a quantity
Express one quantity as a percentage of another
Calculate percentage increase or decrease
Calculate with simple and compound interest
examples: Profit and loss (as amount or percentage)
notes: Formulas are NOT given

subtopics.title: Using a calculator
content: Use a calculator efficiently
Enter values appropriately on a calculator
Interpret the calculator display appropriately
examples: Know not to round values within a calculation
Enter 2 hours 30 minutes as 2.5 hours or 2° 30' 0''

subtopics.title: Time
content: Calculate with time: seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks, months, years
Calculate times in 24-hour and 12-hour clock
Read clocks and timetables
notes: 1 year = 365 days
Includes time zones, local times and time differences

subtopics.title: Money
content: Calculate with money
Convert from one currency to another

subtopics.title: Introduction to algebra
content: Letters can be used to represent generalised numbers
Substitute numbers into expressions and formulas

subtopics.title: Algebraic manipulation
content: Simplify expressions by collecting like terms
Expand products of algebraic expressions
Factorise by extracting common factors
notes: Simplify means give answer in simplest form
Factorise means factorise fully

subtopics.title: Indices II
content: Understand and use indices (positive, zero and negative)
Understand and use the rules of indices
notes: Knowledge of logarithms is NOT required

subtopics.title: Equations
content: Construct simple expressions, equations and formulas
Solve linear equations in one unknown
Solve simultaneous linear equations in two unknowns
Change the subject of simple formulas
examples: Write an expression for a number that is 2 more than n
Change subject where: subject only appears once, no power or root of subject
notes: Includes constructing linear simultaneous equations

subtopics.title: Inequalities
content: Represent and interpret inequalities
Including on a number line
notes: Open circles for strict inequalities (<, >)
Closed circles for inclusive inequalities (≤, ≥)

subtopics.title: Sequences
content: Continue a given number sequence or pattern
Recognise patterns in sequences, including term-to-term rule
Find and use nth term of linear, simple quadratic, and simple cubic sequences

subtopics.title: Graphs in practical situations
content: Use and interpret graphs in practical situations
Travel graphs and conversion graphs
Draw graphs from given data
examples: Interpret gradient of straight-line graph as rate of change
Draw distance-time graph to represent a journey

subtopics.title: Graphs of functions
content: Construct tables of values and draw graphs
Functions: ax + b, ±x² + ax + b, a/x (x ≠ 0)
Solve associated equations graphically
examples: Find intersection of line and curve
notes: a and b are integer constants

subtopics.title: Sketching curves
content: Recognise, sketch and interpret graphs
Linear and quadratic functions
notes: Knowledge of symmetry and roots required
Knowledge of turning points NOT required

subtopics.title: Coordinates
content: Use and interpret Cartesian coordinates in two dimensions

subtopics.title: Drawing linear graphs
content: Draw straight-line graphs for linear equations
notes: Equations given in form y = mx + c (e.g., y = -2x + 5)
Unless table of values is given

subtopics.title: Gradient of linear graphs
content: Find the gradient of a straight line
notes: From a grid only

subtopics.title: Equations of linear graphs
content: Interpret and obtain equation of straight-line graph in form y = mx + c
examples: Use and request lines in forms: y = mx + c, x = k
Find equation when graph is given
Find gradient and y-intercept from equation y = 6x + 3
notes: Give equations in fully simplified form

subtopics.title: Parallel lines
content: Find gradient and equation of straight line parallel to given line
examples: Find equation of line parallel to y = 4x - 1 that passes through (1, -3)

subtopics.title: Geometrical terms
content: Lines and angles
Triangles and quadrilaterals
Polygons
Circles
Simple solids

subtopics.title: Geometrical constructions
content: Measure and draw lines and angles
Construct triangle given all sides using ruler and compasses only
Draw, use and interpret nets
examples: Construct rhombus by drawing two triangles
Draw nets of cubes, cuboids, prisms and pyramids
Use measurements from nets to calculate volumes and surface areas
notes: Ruler used for all straight edges
Construction arcs must be shown
Perpendicular bisectors and angle bisectors NOT required

subtopics.title: Scale drawings
content: Draw and interpret scale drawings
Use and interpret three-figure bearings
examples: Find bearing of A from B if bearing of B from A is 025°
Point D is due east of point C
notes: Ruler must be used for all straight edges
Bearings measured clockwise from north (000° to 360°)
Understanding of north, east, south, west required

subtopics.title: Similarity
content: Calculate lengths of similar shapes

subtopics.title: Symmetry
content: Recognise line symmetry
Order of rotational symmetry in 2D
notes: Includes properties of triangles, quadrilaterals and polygons related to symmetries

subtopics.title: Angles
content: Calculate unknown angles using geometrical properties
Angles formed within parallel lines
Angle properties of regular polygons
properties: Sum of angles at a point = 360°
Sum of angles on straight line = 180°
notes: Three-letter notation for angles (e.g., angle ABC)
Use correct geometrical terminology when giving reasons
Includes exterior and interior angles, and angle sum

subtopics.title: Units of measure
content: Use metric units of mass, length, area, volume and capacity
Convert quantities into larger or smaller units
conversions: Between different units of area (e.g., cm² ↔ m²)
Between units of volume and capacity (e.g., m³ ↔ litres)

subtopics.title: Area and perimeter
content: Perimeter and area of rectangle
Perimeter and area of triangle
Perimeter and area of parallelogram
Perimeter and area of trapezium
notes: Except for area of triangle, formulas are NOT given

subtopics.title: Circles, arcs and sectors
content: Circumference and area of circle
Arc length and sector area as fractions of circumference and area
Sector angle must be factor of 360°
notes: Answers may be asked for in terms of π
Formulas are given in List of formulas

subtopics.title: Surface area and volume
content: Surface area and volume of cuboid
Surface area and volume of prism
Surface area and volume of cylinder
Surface area and volume of sphere
Surface area and volume of pyramid
Surface area and volume of cone
notes: Answers may be asked for in terms of π
Prism refers to any solid with uniform cross-section (e.g., cylindrical sector)

subtopics.title: Pythagoras' theorem
content: Know and use Pythagoras' theorem

subtopics.title: Right-angled triangles
content: Know and use sine, cosine and tangent ratios for acute angles
Calculations involving sides and angles of right-angled triangle
Solve problems in 2D using Pythagoras' theorem and trigonometry
notes: Angles in degrees, answers to one decimal place
Knowledge of bearings may be required

subtopics.title: Transformations
content: Reflection of shape in vertical or horizontal line
Rotation about origin, vertices or midpoints through multiples of 90°
Enlargement from a centre by scale factor
Translation by a vector
notes: No combinations of transformations
Ruler must be used for all straight edges
Positive and fractional scale factors only

subtopics.title: Introduction to probability
content: Understand and use probability scale from 0 to 1
Calculate probability of single event
Probability of event not occurring = 1 - probability of event occurring
examples: The probability that a counter is blue is 0.8. What is the probability that it is not blue?
notes: Probability notation NOT required
Give as fraction, decimal or percentage
May use tables, graphs or Venn diagrams (limited to two sets)

subtopics.title: Relative and expected frequencies
content: Understand relative frequency as estimate of probability
Calculate expected frequencies
examples: Use results of experiments with spinner to estimate probability
Use probability to estimate expected value from population
notes: Includes understanding fair, bias and random

subtopics.title: Probability of combined events
content: Calculate probability using sample space diagrams
Calculate probability using Venn diagrams
Calculate probability using tree diagrams
notes: Combined events only with replacement
Venn diagrams limited to two sets
Tree diagrams: outcomes at end of branches, probabilities by side of branches

subtopics.title: Classifying statistical data
content: Classify and tabulate statistical data

subtopics.title: Interpreting statistical data
content: Read, interpret and draw inferences from tables and statistical diagrams
Compare sets of data using tables, graphs and statistical measures
Appreciate restrictions on drawing conclusions from given data
examples: Compare averages and ranges between two data sets

subtopics.title: Averages and range
content: Calculate mean, median, mode and range for individual data
Distinguish between purposes for which these are used
notes: Data may be in list or frequency table
Data will NOT be grouped

subtopics.title: Statistical charts and diagrams
content: Draw and interpret bar charts
Draw and interpret pie charts
Draw and interpret pictograms
Draw and interpret stem-and-leaf diagrams
Draw and interpret simple frequency distributions
notes: Includes composite (stacked) and dual (side-by-side) bar charts
Stem-and-leaf diagrams should have ordered data with a key

subtopics.title: Scatter diagrams
content: Draw and interpret scatter diagrams
Understand positive, negative and zero correlation
Draw by eye, interpret and use straight line of best fit
notes: Plotted points clearly marked (e.g., small crosses ×)
Line of best fit: single ruled line by inspection, extends across full data set
Roughly even distribution of points either side of line

subtopics.title: Types of number
content: Natural numbers
Integers (positive, zero and negative)
Prime numbers
Square numbers
Cube numbers
Common factors
Common multiples
Rational and irrational numbers
Reciprocals
examples: Convert between numbers and words (e.g., six billion is 6000000000, 10007 is ten thousand and seven)
Express 72 as a product of its prime factors
Find the highest common factor (HCF) of two numbers
Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of two numbers

subtopics.title: Sets
content: Understand and use set language, notation and Venn diagrams to describe sets and represent relationships between sets
Limited to two or three sets
notation: n(A) - Number of elements in set A
A' - Complement of set A
ξ - Universal set
A ∪ B - Union of A and B
A ∩ B - Intersection of A and B
A ⊆ B - A is a subset of B
A ⊈ B - A is not a subset of B
examples: A = {x: x is a natural number}
notes: Venn diagrams are limited to two or three sets
The following set notation will be used: n(A), ∈, ∉, A', ξ, A ⊆ B, A ⊈ B, ∅, A ∪ B, A ∩ B

subtopics.title: Powers and roots
content: Squares and square roots
Cubes and cube roots
Other powers and roots of numbers
required: Recall squares and roots from 1 to 15
Recall cubes and roots of 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and 10

subtopics.title: Fractions, decimals and percentages
content: Proper fractions
Improper fractions
Mixed numbers
Decimals
Percentages
Recognise equivalence and convert between forms
notes: Write fractions in simplest form
NOT expected: recurring decimal notation
NOT expected: converting recurring decimals to fractions

subtopics.title: Ordering
content: Order quantities by magnitude
Use symbols: =, ≠, >, <, ≥, ≤

subtopics.title: The four operations
content: Calculations with integers, fractions and decimals
Correct ordering of operations
Use of brackets
Negative numbers
Improper fractions and mixed numbers

subtopics.title: Indices I
content: Understand and use indices (positive, zero and negative integers)
Understand and use the rules of indices

subtopics.title: Standard form
content: Use standard form A × 10ⁿ where n is positive or negative integer and 1 ≤ A < 10
Convert numbers into and out of standard form
Calculate with values in standard form
notes: Core candidates expected to calculate with standard form only on Paper 3

subtopics.title: Estimation
content: Round values to specified degree of accuracy
Make estimates for calculations
Round answers to reasonable degree of accuracy
examples: Write 5764 correct to the nearest thousand
Estimate 41.3/(9.79 × 0.765) by rounding to 1 significant figure
notes: Includes decimal places and significant figures

subtopics.title: Limits of accuracy
content: Give upper and lower bounds for data rounded to specified accuracy
examples: Write down the upper bound of a length measured correct to the nearest metre
notes: NOT expected: bounds of results of calculations using rounded data

subtopics.title: Ratio and proportion
content: Give ratios in simplest form
Divide a quantity in a given ratio
Use proportional reasoning and ratios in context

subtopics.title: Rates
content: Use common measures of rate
Apply other measures of rate
Solve problems involving average speed
examples: A cyclist travels 45km in 3 hours 45 minutes. What is their average speed?
notes: Required formulas given in question
Knowledge of speed/distance/time formula required
Notation: m/s (metres per second), g/cm³ (grams per cubic centimetre)

subtopics.title: Percentages
content: Calculate a given percentage of a quantity
Express one quantity as a percentage of another
Calculate percentage increase or decrease
Calculate with simple and compound interest
examples: Profit and loss (as amount or percentage)
notes: Formulas are NOT given

subtopics.title: Using a calculator
content: Use a calculator efficiently
Enter values appropriately on a calculator
Interpret the calculator display appropriately
examples: Know not to round values within a calculation
Enter 2 hours 30 minutes as 2.5 hours or 2° 30' 0''

subtopics.title: Time
content: Calculate with time: seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks, months, years
Calculate times in 24-hour and 12-hour clock
Read clocks and timetables
notes: 1 year = 365 days
Includes time zones, local times and time differences

subtopics.title: Money
content: Calculate with money
Convert from one currency to another

subtopics.title: Surds
content: Understand and use the rules of surds
Simplify expressions involving surds
Rationalise denominators

subtopics.title: Introduction to algebra
content: Letters can be used to represent generalised numbers
Substitute numbers into expressions and formulas

subtopics.title: Algebraic manipulation
content: Simplify expressions by collecting like terms
Expand products of algebraic expressions
Factorise by extracting common factors
notes: Simplify means give answer in simplest form
Factorise means factorise fully

subtopics.title: Factorisation
content: Factorise where possible expressions of the form: ax² + bx + c
Factorise difference of two squares
Simplify algebraic fractions

subtopics.title: Indices II
content: Understand and use indices (positive, zero and negative)
Understand and use the rules of indices
Use and simplify expressions involving fractional and negative indices
Write roots using fractional indices
notes: Knowledge of logarithms is NOT required

subtopics.title: Equations
content: Construct simple expressions, equations and formulas
Solve linear equations in one unknown
Solve simultaneous linear equations in two unknowns
Change the subject of simple formulas
Solve quadratic equations by factorisation
Solve quadratic equations using the formula
Solve simple exponential equations
Change the subject of more complex formulas
examples: Write an expression for a number that is 2 more than n
Change subject where: subject only appears once, no power or root of subject
Make x the subject of: y = (x + 3)/(x - 2)
notes: Includes constructing linear simultaneous equations

subtopics.title: Inequalities
content: Represent and interpret inequalities
Including on a number line
Solve simple linear inequalities in one variable
Represent linear inequalities in two variables graphically
notes: Open circles for strict inequalities (<, >)
Closed circles for inclusive inequalities (≤, ≥)

subtopics.title: Sequences
content: Continue a given number sequence or pattern
Recognise patterns in sequences, including term-to-term rule
Find and use nth term of linear, simple quadratic, and simple cubic sequences

subtopics.title: Proportion
content: Direct proportion: y ∝ x and y = kx
Inverse proportion: y ∝ 1/x and y = k/x
Recognise and solve problems involving direct and inverse proportion
examples: y is directly proportional to x. When x = 4, y = 12. Find y when x = 7
y is inversely proportional to x. When x = 2, y = 6. Find y when x = 3

subtopics.title: Graphs in practical situations
content: Use and interpret graphs in practical situations
Travel graphs and conversion graphs
Draw graphs from given data
examples: Interpret gradient of straight-line graph as rate of change
Draw distance-time graph to represent a journey

subtopics.title: Graphs of functions
content: Construct tables of values and draw graphs
Functions: ax + b, ±x² + ax + b, a/x (x ≠ 0)
Solve associated equations graphically
examples: Find intersection of line and curve
notes: a and b are integer constants

subtopics.title: Sketching curves
content: Construct tables of values and draw graphs for functions
Functions include: ax^n where n = -2, -1, -1/2, 0, 1/2, 1, 2, 3
Functions include: a^x for positive values of x
Estimate gradients of curves by drawing tangents
notes: Reciprocal graphs: vertical asymptote where denominator = 0
Exponential graphs: y-intercept is the coefficient, horizontal asymptote at y = 0
Drawing tangents for gradient estimation

subtopics.title: Function notation
content: Use function notation
Find composite functions
Find inverse functions
notation: f(x) - function notation
fg(x) or f(g(x)) - composite function
f⁻¹(x) - inverse function
examples: If f(x) = x² and g(x) = x + 1, find fg(x)

subtopics.title: Domain and range
content: Understand and use the terms domain and range
Identify domain and range from context or graph
examples: State the domain and range of f(x) = √x
State the range of f(x) = x² for domain -2 ≤ x ≤ 3
notes: Domain: set of possible input values
Range: set of possible output values

subtopics.title: Coordinates
content: Use and interpret Cartesian coordinates in two dimensions

subtopics.title: Drawing linear graphs
content: Draw straight-line graphs for linear equations
notes: Equations given in form y = mx + c (e.g., y = -2x + 5)
Unless table of values is given

subtopics.title: Gradient of linear graphs
content: Find the gradient of a straight line
notes: From a grid only

subtopics.title: Coordinate geometry calculations
content: Find the gradient of a straight line from coordinates of two points
Find the length of a line segment
Find the midpoint of a line segment
Find the equation of a straight line given sufficient information
examples: Find gradient of line through (1, 2) and (4, 8)
Find length of line joining (-1, 3) and (2, 7)
Find midpoint of line joining (-2, 5) and (4, 1)

subtopics.title: Equations of linear graphs
content: Interpret and obtain equation of straight-line graph in form y = mx + c
examples: Use and request lines in forms: y = mx + c, x = k
Find equation when graph is given
Find gradient and y-intercept from equation y = 6x + 3
notes: Give equations in fully simplified form

subtopics.title: Parallel lines
content: Find gradient and equation of straight line parallel to given line
examples: Find equation of line parallel to y = 4x - 1 that passes through (1, -3)

subtopics.title: Perpendicular lines
content: Understand that perpendicular lines have gradients m₁ and m₂ such that m₁ × m₂ = -1
Find equation of perpendicular line
examples: Find equation of line perpendicular to y = 2x + 1 passing through (4, 3)
notes: Product of gradients of perpendicular lines = -1

subtopics.title: Geometrical terms
content: Lines and angles
Triangles and quadrilaterals
Polygons
Circles
Simple solids

subtopics.title: Geometrical constructions
content: Measure and draw lines and angles
Construct triangle given all sides using ruler and compasses only
Draw, use and interpret nets
examples: Construct rhombus by drawing two triangles
Draw nets of cubes, cuboids, prisms and pyramids
Use measurements from nets to calculate volumes and surface areas
notes: Ruler used for all straight edges
Construction arcs must be shown
Perpendicular bisectors and angle bisectors NOT required

subtopics.title: Scale drawings
content: Draw and interpret scale drawings
Use and interpret three-figure bearings
examples: Find bearing of A from B if bearing of B from A is 025°
Point D is due east of point C
notes: Ruler must be used for all straight edges
Bearings measured clockwise from north (000° to 360°)
Understanding of north, east, south, west required

subtopics.title: Similarity
content: Calculate lengths of similar shapes

subtopics.title: Symmetry
content: Recognise line symmetry
Order of rotational symmetry in 2D
notes: Includes properties of triangles, quadrilaterals and polygons related to symmetries

subtopics.title: Angles
content: Calculate unknown angles using geometrical properties
Angles formed within parallel lines
Angle properties of regular polygons
properties: Sum of angles at a point = 360°
Sum of angles on straight line = 180°
notes: Three-letter notation for angles (e.g., angle ABC)
Use correct geometrical terminology when giving reasons
Includes exterior and interior angles, and angle sum

subtopics.title: Circle theorems
content: Know and use circle theorem properties
Solve problems using circle theorems
properties: Angle in a semicircle is a right angle
Angle at centre is twice angle at circumference
Angles in same segment are equal
Opposite angles of cyclic quadrilateral sum to 180°
Angle between tangent and radius is 90°
Tangents from external point are equal length
notes: Proofs of circle theorems are NOT required
Use correct geometrical terminology when giving reasons

subtopics.title: Units of measure
content: Use metric units of mass, length, area, volume and capacity
Convert quantities into larger or smaller units
conversions: Between different units of area (e.g., cm² ↔ m²)
Between units of volume and capacity (e.g., m³ ↔ litres)

subtopics.title: Area and perimeter
content: Perimeter and area of rectangle
Perimeter and area of triangle
Perimeter and area of parallelogram
Perimeter and area of trapezium
Area of compound shapes
notes: Except for area of triangle, formulas are NOT given
examples: Find area of shape with circular and rectangular parts

subtopics.title: Circles, arcs and sectors
content: Circumference and area of circle
Arc length as fraction of circumference
Sector area as fraction of circle area
Solve problems involving arcs and sectors
notes: Answers may be asked for in terms of π
Formulas are given in List of formulas
Sector angle NOT restricted to factors of 360°

subtopics.title: Surface area and volume
content: Surface area and volume of cuboid
Surface area and volume of prism
Surface area and volume of cylinder
Surface area and volume of sphere
Surface area and volume of pyramid
Surface area and volume of cone
Surface area and volume of composite solids
Surface area and volume of hemisphere
notes: Answers may be asked for in terms of π
Prism refers to any solid with uniform cross-section (e.g., cylindrical sector)

subtopics.title: Pythagoras' theorem
content: Know and use Pythagoras' theorem

subtopics.title: Right-angled triangles
content: Know and use sine, cosine and tangent ratios for acute and obtuse angles
Solve problems in 2D and 3D using Pythagoras' theorem and trigonometry
Calculate lengths and angles in three-dimensional situations
notes: Angles in degrees, answers to one decimal place
Knowledge of bearings may be required
Includes problems with angles of elevation and depression

subtopics.title: Sine and cosine rules
content: Know and use the sine rule: a/sin A = b/sin B = c/sin C
Know and use the cosine rule: a² = b² + c² - 2bc cos A
Use sine and cosine rules to solve problems in 2D
Calculate area of triangle using ½ab sin C
formulas_given: Area of triangle = ½ab sin C
notes: Triangles may be acute-angled or obtuse-angled
Choose appropriate rule based on given information

subtopics.title: Exact trigonometric values
content: Know and use exact values of sin, cos, tan for 0°, 30°, 45°, 60°, 90°
notes: Values should be known exactly, not as decimals

subtopics.title: Transformations
content: Reflection of shape in any line (including y = x, y = -x)
Rotation about any centre through any angle
Enlargement from any centre by any scale factor (positive, negative, fractional)
Translation by a vector
Stretch parallel to an axis
Combined transformations
notes: Ruler must be used for all straight edges
Includes negative and fractional scale factors
Combined transformations: apply in order stated
Stretch has scale factor parallel to one axis

subtopics.title: Vectors
content: Describe a translation using a vector
Add and subtract vectors
Multiply a vector by a scalar
Calculate the magnitude of a vector
Represent vectors using directed line segments
Use position vectors
notation: Vector AB with arrow notation
Column vector notation: (x, y)
|a| - magnitude of vector a
Position vector: vector from origin to a point
examples: If a = (3, 2) and b = (1, -4), find a + b

subtopics.title: Introduction to probability
content: Understand and use probability scale from 0 to 1
Calculate probability of single event
Probability of event not occurring = 1 - probability of event occurring
examples: The probability that a counter is blue is 0.8. What is the probability that it is not blue?
notes: Give as fraction, decimal or percentage
May use tables, graphs or Venn diagrams (limited to two or three sets)
notation: P(A) - probability of event A
P(A') - probability of event A not occurring
P(A ∪ B) - probability of A or B
P(A ∩ B) - probability of A and B

subtopics.title: Relative and expected frequencies
content: Understand relative frequency as estimate of probability
Calculate expected frequencies
examples: Use results of experiments with spinner to estimate probability
Use probability to estimate expected value from population
notes: Includes understanding fair, bias and random

subtopics.title: Probability of combined events
content: Calculate probability using sample space diagrams
Calculate probability using Venn diagrams (two or three sets)
Calculate probability using tree diagrams
Calculate conditional probability
Understand mutually exclusive and independent events
notes: Combined events with and without replacement
Venn diagrams limited to two or three sets
Tree diagrams: outcomes at end of branches, probabilities by side of branches
Conditional probability: P(A|B) means probability of A given B has occurred
notation: P(A|B) - conditional probability

subtopics.title: Classifying statistical data
content: Classify and tabulate statistical data

subtopics.title: Interpreting statistical data
content: Read, interpret and draw inferences from tables and statistical diagrams
Compare sets of data using tables, graphs and statistical measures
Appreciate restrictions on drawing conclusions from given data
examples: Compare averages and ranges between two data sets

subtopics.title: Averages and range
content: Calculate mean, median, mode and range for individual and discrete data
Calculate mean for grouped data
Identify the modal class for grouped data
Calculate and use estimated mean for grouped data
notes: Data may be in list, frequency table, or grouped frequency table
For grouped data, use midpoint of class interval

subtopics.title: Statistical charts and diagrams
content: Draw and interpret bar charts
Draw and interpret pie charts
Draw and interpret pictograms
Draw and interpret stem-and-leaf diagrams
Draw and interpret simple frequency distributions
Draw and interpret histograms with equal class intervals
Draw and interpret cumulative frequency diagrams
Estimate median, quartiles and interquartile range from cumulative frequency
notes: Includes composite (stacked) and dual (side-by-side) bar charts
Stem-and-leaf diagrams should have ordered data with a key
Histograms: area represents frequency, all class intervals equal width
Cumulative frequency: plot at upper class boundary
Quartiles: Q1 at 25%, median at 50%, Q3 at 75%
Interquartile range = Q3 - Q1

subtopics.title: Scatter diagrams
content: Draw and interpret scatter diagrams
Understand positive, negative and zero correlation
Draw by eye, interpret and use straight line of best fit
notes: Plotted points clearly marked (e.g., small crosses ×)
Line of best fit: single ruled line by inspection, extends across full data set
Roughly even distribution of points either side of line
